hints.mitte: 'mitte' is a verb but it does not appear in this phrase — 'Dimitte' does
hints.noli: 'noli' means 'do not' but does not appear in this phrase

hints.to send: 'to send' is 'mittere' — 'Dimitte' comes from 'dimittere' (to release/let go)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'dimittit' — 'Dimitte' is a command
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'dimittat' — 'Dimitte' is the imperative (a direct command)
hints.infinitive: Infinitive would be 'dimittere' — 'Dimitte' is the imperative singular

hints.mitto, mittere, misi, missum: These are principal parts of 'mitto' (to send) — 'dimitto' is the compound form
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are principal parts of 'do' (to give)
hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are principal parts of 'duco' (to lead)

hints.nominative: 'nos' can be nominative (we) but here it is the object of 'dimitte' — accusative
hints.dative: Dative of 1st person plural is 'nobis' — 'nos' is accusative here
hints.ablative: Ablative of 1st person plural is 'nobis' — 'nos' is accusative here
